message:
feat(core,vdbe,vfs): planner-directed query lowering, trigger column resolution fix, GROUP BY + window functions, recursive triggers, io_uring status snapshots

Major cross-crate enhancement integrating the query planner into VDBE
code generation and fixing several correctness issues discovered via
conformance oracle testing.

Query planner integration (connection.rs + codegen.rs):

- Imports from fsqlite_planner: best_access_path, decompose_where,
  classify_where_term, and supporting types (AccessPathKind, IndexInfo,
  TableStats, StatsSource, WhereTermKind).

- New codegen types: PlannerSelectAccessKind (FullTableScan, RowidLookup,
  IndexEquality, IndexRange) and SelectPlannerDirective — the planner
  produces a directive specifying the optimal single-table access path,
  which codegen either honors or emits an explicit bypass reason before
  falling back to heuristic selection.

- Directive validation: directive_index_contract_bypass_reason() checks
  table name match, leading index column alignment, and covering-index
  eligibility. Mismatches are logged with structured tracing fields
  (run_id, trace_id, scenario_id, plan_id, honor_mode, bypass_reason)
  for post-hoc planner accuracy analysis.

- Four new codegen paths: codegen_select_full_scan,
  codegen_select_rowid_lookup, codegen_select_index_equality, and
  codegen_select_index_range, each handling the planner's directive
  with proper cursor management and filter residuals.

Trigger column resolution fix (connection.rs):

- TriggerFrame::column_matches_prefix() now returns false for bare
  column references (no table prefix). Previously, `None => true`
  caused `WHERE id = NEW.id` to become `WHERE 1 = 1` — both sides
  were replaced with the same literal, updating ALL rows instead of
  just the targeted one. Now only explicitly prefixed references
  (NEW.col, OLD.col) resolve to trigger pseudo-column values.

GROUP BY + window function two-phase execution (connection.rs):

- New execute_group_by_window_select path handles SELECT statements
  that combine GROUP BY with window functions (ROW_NUMBER, RANK,
  SUM OVER, etc.). Phase 1 performs GROUP BY aggregation; Phase 2
  applies window functions to the grouped result. Previously these
  queries would fail or produce incorrect results.

PRAGMA recursive_triggers (connection.rs):

- When recursive_triggers is OFF (the default, matching SQLite), a
  trigger body that modifies the same table no longer re-fires triggers
  on that table. Implemented by checking the trigger_frame_stack for
  same-table entries before both BEFORE and AFTER trigger dispatch.

sqlite_master materialization root page collision (connection.rs + engine.rs):

- The temp table used for materialized sqlite_master now probes the
  pager to ensure its root page doesn't collide with stale data from
  previously dropped tables. New MemDatabase::next_root_page() and
  set_next_root_page() accessors allow advancing past occupied pages.
  Also invalidates cached VDBE programs for sqlite_master queries
  since root pages differ between materializations.

io_uring VFS status snapshots (uring.rs):

- New IoUringRuntimeStatus struct with backend, available, disabled,
  initial_status, status, and disable_reason fields. OnceLock-based
  disable reason tracking preserves the first disable cause. Exposed
  via IoUringVfs::status_snapshot() and PagerBackend::io_uring_status()
  for PRAGMA-level runtime introspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
